```
⌊m / p^i⌋ + ⌊n / p^i⌋ + ⌊(m + n) / p^i⌋ ≤ 2⌊m / p^i⌋ + 2⌊n / p^i⌋ ≤ ⌊2m / p^i⌋ + ⌊2n / p^i⌋
```
which proves the required inequality. 

But for the case when `p^i > m + n`, all the floors are zero, so the inequality holds trivially. 

For the other cases, it's as above. 

Therefore, using

have h_main : ∀ (p : ℕ), p.Prime → (∑ k in Finset.Icc 1 (2 * m), (2 * m) / p ^ k) + (∑ k in Finset.Icc 1 (2 * n), (2 * n) / p ^ k) ≥ (∑ k in Finset.Icc 1 m, m / p ^ k) + (∑ k in Finset.Icc 1 n, n / p ^ k) + (∑ k in Finset.Icc 1 (m + n), (m + n) / p ^ k) := by sorry
  have h_final : m ! * n ! * (m + n)! ∣ (2 * m)! * (2 * n)! := by sorry
  exact h_final
